content: We can model the behavior of any general agent (per its definition as an entity that exerts power to produce effects) using a sigmoid activation framework, where an agent only produces a tangible downstream effect when its accumulated internal motivation or external stimulus crosses a threshold, rather than triggering action linearly with input strength. This framework explains why many agents (from biological pathogens to goal-driven AI agents) remain dormant until stimulus reaches a critical intensity, then quickly scale to their full effect.
